steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. It's given that $f(x)=x+6$. Substituting $2$ for $x$ in the function $f$ yields $f(2)=2+6$, or $f(2)=8$. It's also given that $g(x)=6x$. Substituting $2$ for $x$ in the function $g$ yields $g(2)=(6)(2)$, or $g\left(2\right)=12$. Substituting $8$ for $f(2)$ and $12$ for $g(2)$ in the expression $7f(2)-g(2)$ yields $7(8)-12$, or $44$. Therefore, the value of $7f(2)-g(2)$ is $44$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $f(2)-g(2)$, not $7f(2)-g(2)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $f(2)$, not $7f(2)-g(2)$.

steps.content: Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
